definitions.text: The variation of strong and weak elements (such as duration, accent) of sounds, notably in speech or music, over time; a beat or meter.
examples: Dance to the rhythm of the music.
The audience clapped in rhythm as I sang.

definitions.text: A specifically defined pattern of such variation.
examples: Most dances have a rhythm as distinctive as the Iambic verse in poetry
Once you get the rhythm of it, the job will become easy.
